title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values (i.e., from left to right, level by level).
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000]
thinking_questions: You need to process nodes level by level — what traversal strategy naturally works level by level?
How do you know which nodes belong to the current level vs the next?
How do you process exactly one level at a time from a queue?

hints.L1: BFS naturally processes level by level. Use a queue and process all nodes at the current level before moving to the next.
hints.L2: Queue initialized with root. For each level, process len(queue) nodes (that's the current level size), collect values, add children.
hints.L3:
# INSIGHT: BFS level-by-level; snapshot queue size at start of each level
from collections import deque
result = []
if not root: return result
q = deque([root])
while q:
    level = []
    for _ in range(len(q)):  # process exactly this level
        node = q.popleft()
        level.append(node.val)
        if node.left: q.append(node.left)
        if node.right: q.append(node.right)
    result.append(level)
return result
# Time: O(n)  Space: O(n)

follow_up_questions: Why does `for _ in range(len(q))` correctly isolate one level?
How would you do reverse level order traversal? (LC 107)
How would you do zigzag level order traversal? (LC 103)
solution_notes: BFS with queue. Snapshot len(q) at start of each level to process one level at a time. O(n).
